extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50), which requires the conjunction of 10 conditions including renal_impairment=true. rec_drug_b is set true by rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50) whenever renal_impairment=true. Since any input that makes rec_drug_a true necessarily has renal_impairment=true, that same input also fires rec_b_from_trigger, making rec_drug_b true. No higher-priority rule unsets either output, and defaults apply only to outputs left unset (both are set true here). Setting all 10 conjunction inputs to true (asthma/anticoagulated/polypharmacy false, irrelevant to a and b) yields rec_drug_a=true and rec_drug_b=true simultaneously, violating the mutual-exclusion property (rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b not jointly true).
